threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus at the stop instead of playing the game of spotting which one will actually show up next.
Heard a group of teens on the 3 bus debating if the Brixton Academy's better at midnight or 3am. Their absolute passion for the scene was wild.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a dude serenading his cat on the windowsill while the bus was delayed for the third time that week. Classic Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you grab a Kebab and watch a full-on street art battle unfold right in front of you. It’s a wild mix of food and talent!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know the secret back entrance to Bi-Rite when the line gets crazy. Tourists are still out front waiting for scoops.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in full tuxedo with a bouquet of roses on the 47 bus. Turns out he was late for his own wedding across town. Pure SF magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the Gràcia street festivals are all about the parades. It's really about the late-night jam sessions on balconies that you have to experience.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they aggressively search for 'local' Spanish cafés but end up in the tourist traps on Carrer Gran.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check the Tube app before every journey instead of just embracing the bus as your new best mate.
Last night on the 333, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 'Ain't No Mountain High Enough.' Even the driver joined in! Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw an older man arguing with a pigeon over a bread roll outside the bakery. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you get a public transport strike that ends just in time for rush hour. It's like a bizarre choreography no one asked for.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only true locals know to avoid BART at rush hour when the brakes overheat. Tourists line up while we just take the bus or bike.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy pull a full-on opera solo on the 31 while juggling his lunch and bag. Didn't even miss a note when the driver hit a bump.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think our streets are always bustling, but after 10 PM, Gràcia turns into a quiet village—perfect for those who know the secret spots.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they still take photos with their phone in front of every single Gaudí building. It’s like a walking tour on repeat.
